error.signature: ROS2 subscriber receives no messages from publisher despite both running with no errors

verdict.summary: ROS2 QoS (Quality of Service) settings must be compatible between publisher and subscriber. A RELIABLE publisher and BEST_EFFORT subscriber work, but BEST_EFFORT publisher + RELIABLE subscriber silently drops all messages with no error by default.

dead_ends.action: Use default QoS for both publisher and subscriber
dead_ends.why_fails: Default QoS is RELIABLE+VOLATILE for most message types but BEST_EFFORT+VOLATILE for sensor data. Mixing sensor publishers with standard subscribers silently fails.

dead_ends.action: Set both publisher and subscriber to RELIABLE
dead_ends.why_fails: Works but RELIABLE over lossy networks (WiFi, multi-robot) causes message queuing and latency spikes. Sensor data becomes stale.

workarounds.action: Use compatible QoS: publisher reliability >= subscriber reliability
workarounds.how: pub_qos = QoSProfile(reliability=RELIABLE); sub_qos = QoSProfile(reliability=BEST_EFFORT)  # RELIABLE pub -> BEST_EFFORT sub is OK

workarounds.action: Use QoSProfile.sensor_data for all sensor topics consistently
workarounds.how: from rclpy.qos import qos_profile_sensor_data; pub = node.create_publisher(Image, 'camera', qos_profile_sensor_data)

workarounds.action: Use 'ros2 topic info -v /topic' to debug QoS mismatches
workarounds.how: ros2 topic info -v /camera/image  # shows publisher and subscriber QoS profiles side by side
